```markdown
# Ship Announcement — [PRODUCT NAME]

**Ship date:** [SHIP DATE]

## 1. Image (paste-ready nano-banana command)

```bash
gemini --yolo "/generate 'Hand-drawn xkcd-style sketch with soft pink and mauve watercolor washes on a pale-pink background. Wide horizontal 16:9 composition. A small cartoon character stands on tiptoe and presses a giant rubber stamp down onto a [PRODUCT TYPE — laptop / book / app icon / package / certificate]. The stamp leaves a bold hand-lettered SHIPPED mark across the product, with the product name "[PRODUCT NAME]" and ship date "[SHIP DATE]" hand-lettered below the stamp. A small annotation arrow points to the stamp with the handwritten label "(finally)". Loose ink line work, selective soft pink wash, minimal but expressive, whimsical-with-a-touch-of-tragic-relief. Lots of whitespace.' --styles=sketch,watercolor"
```

**If you don't have nano-banana / gemini installed:** strip the `gemini --yolo "/generate '` prefix and the closing `' --styles=sketch,watercolor"`. Paste the inner prompt into any image generator (DALL-E, Midjourney, Ideogram, Flux). The style anchors — hand-drawn xkcd-style, soft pink wash, 16:9 horizontal — carry across tools.

---

## 2. Instagram caption

[150-220 words]

**Structure:** Hook (1 line) → vulnerable beat (2-3 lines) → what-it-is (1 line) → who-it's-for (1 line) → "link in bio" CTA → hashtags (5-8 max).

**Hashtags:** #ShipItWall + 4-7 contextual ones.

**Alt-text (for accessibility):** [one sentence describing the SHIPPED-stamp image]

---

## 3. LinkedIn post

[250-400 words]

**Structure:** Open with specific moment-of-pain that drove the build → identify the audience by behavior → what-it-is (one sentence) → ONE differentiator (not three) → soft CTA (DM me or comment) → link.

---

## 4. Twitter — single-tweet version

[280 chars max]

[Hook + link + 1 hashtag]

## 5. Twitter — 5-tweet thread

1/ [Hook + specific number]

2/ [The problem in their words]

3/ [What we built + 1 differentiator]

4/ [One vulnerable beat]

5/ [Link + DM CTA + #ShipItWall]

---

## 6. Substack / Newsletter post

[600-900 words]

**Structure:** Story-led open (the moment you decided to build this, with a real scene) → the problem you saw → the build itself including 1-2 specific setbacks → what shipped → what it's for → ask (DM me / forward this) → link with Ship It System attribution if used.

---

## 7. /shipped Wall submission (one-click)

**Mailto URL:**
```
mailto:hello@unstuckwithmolly.com?subject=%5BShip%20It%20Wall%5D%20My%20ship&body=[URL-ENCODED-BODY]
```

**Plain-text body (paste manually if mailto fails):**

```
Project name: [PRODUCT NAME]
URL (if public): [LINK]
What it is (one sentence): [ONE-LINE]

Which Kit templates or modules you used: [from User Context Section D]
Ship date: [SHIP DATE]

A one-line tweet draft (we'll polish if you want — leave blank if you'd
rather Molly write one):
[ONE-LINE TWEET FROM SECTION 4]

Anything else you want me to know:

— Permission to feature on the public Ship It Wall? (Yes / Yes anonymized
   / Private — just sharing the win.) Any answer is welcome.
— Want to use #ShipItWall when you post about it?
```
